 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.2","title":"Build Phase Timeline Component","description":"# Build Phase Timeline Component

## Purpose
Create the visual timeline showing session phases and progress.

## Phase Flow
```
intake → sharpening → level_split → exclusion_test → 
object_transpose → scale_check → agent_dispatch → 
synthesis → evidence_gathering → revision → complete
```

## Visual States
- **Complete**: Filled circle (●), green
- **Current**: Half-filled (◐), blue, highlighted
- **Available**: Empty circle (○), gray, clickable
- **Locked**: Dimmed circle, no interaction
- **Skipped**: Strikethrough, dimmed

## Component API
```typescript
interface PhaseTimelineProps {
  phases: SessionPhase[];
  currentPhase: SessionPhase;
  completedPhases: SessionPhase[];
  availablePhases: SessionPhase[];
  skippedPhases: SessionPhase[];
  onPhaseClick: (phase: SessionPhase) => void;
}
```

## Responsive Behavior
- **Desktop**: Horizontal timeline with labels
- **Tablet**: Horizontal timeline, abbreviated labels
- **Mobile**: Vertical timeline or stepper

## Keyboard Navigation
- Arrow keys to navigate phases
- Enter to select phase
- Visual focus indicator

## Animation
- Smooth transition between phases
- Completion animation (checkmark appear)
- Progress indicator animation

## File Location
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/PhaseTimeline.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All phase states displayed correctly
- [ ] Phase switching works
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Responsive layout works
- [ ] Animations are smooth

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.3","title":"Implement Session State Machine","description":"# Implement Session State Machine

## Purpose
Create a formal state machine that governs session phase transitions.

## Why a State Machine
Session progression has complex rules:
- Some phases depend on others
- Some phases can be skipped
- Some phases can be revisited
- Invalid transitions should be impossible

A state machine makes this explicit and prevents bugs.

## State Definition
```typescript
const sessionMachine = createMachine({
  id: 'session',
  initial: 'intake',
  
  states: {
    intake: {
      on: { SUBMIT_HYPOTHESIS: 'sharpening' }
    },
    
    sharpening: {
      on: {
        REFINE: 'sharpening',  // Stay and refine more
        CONTINUE: 'level_split',
        SKIP_OPERATORS: 'agent_dispatch'
      }
    },
    
    level_split: {
      on: {
        COMPLETE: 'exclusion_test',
        SKIP: 'exclusion_test',
        BACK: 'sharpening'
      }
    },
    
    exclusion_test: {
      on: {
        COMPLETE: 'object_transpose',
        SKIP: 'object_transpose',
        BACK: 'level_split'
      }
    },
    
    object_transpose: {
      on: {
        COMPLETE: 'scale_check',
        SKIP: 'scale_check',
        BACK: 'exclusion_test'
      }
    },
    
    scale_check: {
      on: {
        COMPLETE: 'agent_dispatch',
        SKIP: 'agent_dispatch',
        BACK: 'object_transpose'
      }
    },
    
    agent_dispatch: {
      on: {
        RESPONSES_RECEIVED: 'synthesis',
        SKIP_AGENTS: 'evidence_gathering',
        BACK: 'scale_check'
      }
    },
    
    synthesis: {
      on: {
        CONTINUE: 'evidence_gathering',
        BACK: 'agent_dispatch'
      }
    },
    
    evidence_gathering: {
      on: {
        ADD_EVIDENCE: 'evidence_gathering',
        REVISE_HYPOTHESIS: 'revision',
        COMPLETE: 'complete',
        BACK: 'synthesis'
      }
    },
    
    revision: {
      on: {
        SAVE: 'evidence_gathering',
        RESTART_OPERATORS: 'level_split'
      }
    },
    
    complete: {
      type: 'final'
    }
  }
});
```

## Integration
Use XState or similar, or custom implementation:
```typescript
function useSessionMachine(initialState: SessionPhase) {
  // Returns current state, available transitions, send function
}
```

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/session-machine.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All states and transitions defined
- [ ] Invalid transitions impossible
- [ ] State persists with session
- [ ] Hook provides clean API

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.4","title":"Create Session List & Management Page","description":"# Create Session List & Management Page

## Purpose
Build the page where users see all their sessions and can create new ones.

## Page Layout
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  BrennerBot Lab                           [+ New Session]       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  Your Research Sessions                                         │
│  ═══════════════════════                                        │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ SM-2024-003                              2 hours ago    │   │
│  │ "Algorithm-driven content causes teen depression"       │   │
│  │ Phase: Evidence Gathering │ Confidence: 37% │ v3        │   │
│  │ [Continue] [Export] [Delete]                            │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ SM-2024-002                              1 day ago      │   │
│  │ "Meditation reduces anxiety through attention training"  │   │
│  │ Phase: Complete │ Confidence: 72% │ v2                  │   │
│  │ [View Brief] [Export] [Delete]                          │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ SM-2024-001                              3 days ago     │   │
│  │ "Remote work improves productivity"                     │   │
│  │ Phase: Exclusion Test │ Confidence: 65% │ v1            │   │
│  │ [Continue] [Export] [Delete]                            │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  [Load More]                                                    │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Features

### Session Card
Shows:
- Session ID
- Hypothesis preview
- Current phase
- Confidence score
- Last updated
- Quick actions

### Filtering & Sorting
- Sort by: Date, Confidence, Phase
- Filter by: Status (active, complete), Confidence range

### Actions
- **Continue**: Resume in-progress session
- **View Brief**: For complete sessions
- **Export**: Download as Markdown/PDF/JSON
- **Delete**: With confirmation

### New Session Flow
Click "+ New Session" → Hypothesis Intake form

## Route
`/lab` or `/sessions`

## File Location
`apps/web/src/app/lab/page.tsx`
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/SessionCard.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions listed with all info
- [ ] Sorting and filtering work
- [ ] Actions work correctly
- [ ] Delete has confirmation
- [ ] Pagination/infinite scroll works

-{"id":"brenner_bot-reew.5","title":"Implement Mobile-Responsive Session Experience","description":"# Implement Mobile-Responsive Session Experience

## Purpose
Ensure the full session experience works well on mobile devices.

## Mobile Challenges
1. Limited horizontal space
2. Touch-based interaction
3. Keyboard covers content
4. Multiple panels don't fit side-by-side

## Mobile Adaptations

### Phase Timeline
- Switch to vertical stepper
- Or compact horizontal with icons only
- Swipe to navigate phases

### Session Dashboard
- Stack panels vertically
- Hypothesis card as collapsible header
- Brenner quotes in expandable section

### Operator Sessions
- Full-width content area
- Step navigation via swipe or buttons
- Quote sidebar becomes bottom sheet

### Agent Tribunal
- Agents in accordion/tabs
- One agent visible at a time
- Expand to see full response

### Evidence Recording
- Full-screen modal
- Multi-step as swipeable pages

## Implementation Approach
- Use Tailwind responsive utilities
- Consider mobile-first CSS
- Test on real devices

## Touch Interactions
- Swipe to navigate
- Long-press for context menus
- Pull-to-refresh for agent updates

## File Location
Various components with responsive variants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All phases work on mobile
- [ ] No horizontal scrolling required
- [ ] Touch interactions feel native
- [ ] Text is readable
- [ ] Actions are tappable

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.6","title":"Add Keyboard Navigation & Accessibility","description":"# Add Keyboard Navigation & Accessibility

## Purpose
Make the session experience fully accessible via keyboard and screen readers.

## Keyboard Navigation

### Global Shortcuts
- `?`: Show keyboard shortcuts
- `Esc`: Close modal/go back
- `Cmd/Ctrl + S`: Save session
- `Cmd/Ctrl + E`: Export

### Phase Navigation
- `←` `→`: Previous/Next phase
- `1-9`: Jump to phase by number
- `Tab`: Navigate within phase

### Within Forms
- `Tab`: Next field
- `Shift+Tab`: Previous field
- `Enter`: Submit/Continue
- `Space`: Toggle checkboxes

### Hypothesis Card
- `E`: Edit hypothesis
- `H`: View history
- `V`: Expand/collapse

## Focus Management
- Clear focus indicators (visible rings)
- Focus trapped in modals
- Focus restored after modal close
- Skip links for screen readers

## Screen Reader Support
- Semantic HTML (headings, landmarks)
- ARIA labels for interactive elements
- Live regions for dynamic updates
- Descriptive alt text

## Color Contrast
- All text meets WCAG AA
- States distinguishable without color
- High contrast mode support

## Testing
- Test with VoiceOver (Mac)
- Test with NVDA (Windows)
- Test keyboard-only navigation
- Run axe-core accessibility audit

## File Location
Various components with accessibility improvements.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All interactions keyboard-accessible
- [ ] Focus management correct
- [ ] Screen reader tested
- [ ] axe-core passes

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.7","title":"FEATURE: Session Templates - Quick Check, Full Analysis, Literature Review","description":"# Session Templates - Quick Check, Full Analysis, Literature Review

## The Gap This Fills
Currently, all sessions follow the same full flow. But users have different needs:
- Quick sanity check of an idea
- Full rigorous analysis
- Literature-focused evaluation
- Design-focused session

Different goals need different session structures.

## Feature Design

### Session Template Structure
```typescript
interface SessionTemplate {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  icon: string;
  
  // Phase configuration
  requiredPhases: SessionPhase[];
  optionalPhases: SessionPhase[];
  skippedPhases: SessionPhase[];
  
  // Default settings
  agentsToDispatch: AgentRole[];
  defaultDepth: 'quick' | 'standard' | 'deep';
  
  // Guidance
  expectedDuration: string;
  bestFor: string[];
  
  // Customization
  allowCustomization: boolean;
}
```

### Pre-Built Templates

#### 1. Quick Check (15-30 min)
```
Purpose: Fast sanity check of an idea
Phases: intake → exclusion_test → synthesis
Agents: Devil's Advocate only
Best for: Early-stage ideas, gut checks

"Is this idea worth pursuing? Get quick pushback."
```

#### 2. Full Analysis (2-4 hours)
```
Purpose: Rigorous comprehensive analysis
Phases: All phases enabled
Agents: All agents
Best for: Important hypotheses, formal research

"Leave no stone unturned. Full Brenner treatment."
```

#### 3. Literature Review (1-2 hours)
```
Purpose: Evaluate hypothesis against existing research
Phases: intake → exclusion_test → evidence_gathering
Agents: None (or just Experiment Designer for search guidance)
Best for: When you want to check existing literature

"What does the literature already say about this?"
```

#### 4. Design Focus (1-2 hours)
```
Purpose: Design experiments to test hypothesis
Phases: intake → level_split → exclusion_test → agent_dispatch
Agents: Experiment Designer only
Best for: Planning research, grant proposals

"What experiments would test this properly?"
```

#### 5. Adversarial Deep Dive (2-3 hours)
```
Purpose: Maximum adversarial pressure
Phases: intake → all operators → agent_dispatch → debate
Agents: All agents + debate mode
Best for: High-stakes hypotheses, preparing for critics

"Try to destroy this hypothesis before critics do."
```

### Template Selection UI
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Choose Session Type                                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  ⚡ QUICK CHECK                          ~20 min           │
│  Fast sanity check. Is this worth pursuing?                │
│  [Select]                                                   │
│                                                             │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                             │
│  🔬 FULL ANALYSIS                        ~3 hours          │
│  Complete Brenner Loop treatment. All operators & agents.  │
│  [Select]                                                   │
│                                                             │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                             │
│  📚 LITERATURE REVIEW                    ~90 min           │
│  Evaluate against existing research.                       │
│  [Select]                                                   │
│                                                             │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                             │
│  🧪 DESIGN FOCUS                         ~90 min           │
│  Plan experiments to test hypothesis.                       │
│  [Select]                                                   │
│                                                             │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                             │
│  ⚔️ ADVERSARIAL DEEP DIVE                ~2 hours          │
│  Maximum challenge. Try to destroy this hypothesis.        │
│  [Select]                                                   │
│                                                             │
│  [Custom Session...] (configure your own phases)            │
│                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Integration Points
- Session Start → Template selection
- State Machine → Configured by template
- Phase Timeline → Shows template-specific phases
- Research Brief → Notes which template was used

## Why This is Critical
Without templates:
- Users face intimidating full flow for simple questions
- Quick checks take too long
- Different use cases force same process

With templates:
- Right-sized process for each need
- Lower barrier to entry
- More sessions run (users aren't scared off)
- Better fit between goal and method

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 5+ templates available
- [ ] Template configures state machine
- [ ] Users can create custom templates
- [ ] Template choice recorded in session
- [ ] Can change template mid-session (with warning)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.8","title":"FEATURE: Guided Coach Mode - Enhanced Scaffolding for New Users","description":"# Guided Coach Mode - Enhanced Scaffolding for New Users

## The Gap This Fills
New users may be overwhelmed by:
- The methodology itself
- What each operator does
- How to formulate good hypotheses
- What "discriminative testing" means

Need a "training wheels" mode that teaches as it guides.

## Feature Design

### Coach Mode Overlay
```typescript
interface CoachSettings {
  enabled: boolean;
  level: 'beginner' | 'intermediate' | 'advanced';
  
  // What to show
  showExamples: boolean;
  showExplanations: boolean;
  showBrenerQuotes: boolean;
  showProgressTips: boolean;
  
  // Interactivity
  pauseForExplanation: boolean;
  requireConfirmation: boolean;
}
```

### Key Features

#### 1. Inline Explanations
Every concept gets an explanation:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  EXCLUSION TEST                                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  💡 COACH: What is an Exclusion Test?                       │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ Most research asks: "Is there evidence for my idea?" │ │
│  │                                                        │ │
│  │ The Exclusion Test asks: "What would PROVE ME WRONG?" │ │
│  │                                                        │ │
│  │ This is Brenner's key insight: Good hypotheses make   │ │
│  │ bold predictions that could fail. If you can't        │ │
│  │ imagine being proven wrong, your hypothesis isn't     │ │
│  │ scientific — it's just an opinion.                    │ │
│  │                                                        │ │
│  │ "I'm going to help you generate tests that could     │ │
│  │  actually falsify your hypothesis."                   │ │
│  │                                                        │ │
│  │ [Got it!] [Show me an example] [Why does this matter?]│ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                             │
│  [Continue to Exclusion Test...]                            │
│                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### 2. Worked Examples
For each phase, show a complete example:
- "Here's how a psychology hypothesis went through Level Split"
- "This is what a good exclusion test looks like"
- "See how this hypothesis was refined after agent feedback"

Example bank organized by domain:
- Psychology examples
- Epidemiology examples
- Economics examples
- etc.

#### 3. Quality Checkpoints
Coach pauses to check understanding:
```
💡 COACH: Let's check your hypothesis formulation

Your hypothesis: "Social media causes depression"

Issues I see:
⚠️ No specific mechanism proposed
⚠️ "Social media" is too broad (which platforms? what behavior?)
⚠️ "Depression" unspecified (clinical? self-reported?)

Would you like help refining this?
[Yes, help me] [No, I'll continue as-is]
```

#### 4. Progress Encouragement
Celebrate learning moments:
- "Great! You identified a falsification condition."
- "You just applied Object Transpose — considering alternatives is crucial."
- "This is a strong exclusion test — high discriminative power."

#### 5. Mistake Prevention
Warn before common errors:
- "You're about to skip Level Split. 40% of failed hypotheses had confounded levels."
- "Your falsification condition sounds like 'supporting evidence' — flip it around."
- "This test has low discriminative power. Consider a stronger test."

#### 6. Gradual Scaffolding Removal
As user completes sessions:
- First 3 sessions: Full coach mode
- Sessions 4-10: Reduced explanations
- After 10: Coach available on demand

Track which explanations user has seen:
- Don't repeat explanations unnecessarily
- Remember user's level
- Adapt to demonstrated competence

### Coach Personas (Future)
Different coaching styles:
- "Sydney Brenner" - Blunt and challenging
- "Supportive Teacher" - Patient and encouraging  
- "Research Mentor" - Professional and thorough

## Why This is Critical
Without coaching:
- New users don't understand the methodology
- They make avoidable mistakes
- The system feels intimidating
- Dropout rate is high

With coaching:
- Learning is built into the experience
- Users understand WHY, not just WHAT
- Mistakes are caught early
- Users develop genuine capability

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Coach mode toggleable
- [ ] Inline explanations for all concepts
- [ ] Worked examples for each phase

 {"id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","title":"Task: Define Test Record and Execution Schemas","description":"# Task: Define Test Record and Execution Schemas

## Objective
Create TypeScript types and Zod schemas for test records, predictions, and execution tracking.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/test-binding.ts`

### Core Schemas
```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

export const TestStatusSchema = z.enum([
  'designed',     // Test designed but not yet started
  'in_progress',  // Test is being executed
  'completed',    // Test has results
  'blocked',      // Test cannot proceed (resources, prerequisites)
]);

export const PredictionSchema = z.object({
  hypothesisId: z.string(),
  prediction: z.string().min(5),  // What this hypothesis predicts
  rationale: z.string().optional(),  // Why this prediction follows
});

export const TestRecordSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().regex(/^T-[A-Z0-9]+-\d{3}$/),
  description: z.string().min(10),
  targetHypotheses: z.array(PredictionSchema).min(1),
  designedInSession: z.string(),
  designedBy: z.string().optional(),
  status: TestStatusSchema,
  methodology: z.string().optional(),  // How to execute the test
  resources: z.string().optional(),  // What's needed
  execution: z.lazy(() => TestExecutionSchema).optional(),
  createdAt: z.string().datetime(),
  updatedAt: z.string().datetime(),
});

export const ConfidenceSchema = z.enum(['high', 'medium', 'low']);

export const TestExecutionSchema = z.object({
  executedAt: z.string().datetime(),
  executedBy: z.string().optional(),
  result: z.string().min(5),  // The raw observation/finding
  matchedPredictions: z.array(z.string()),  // hypothesis IDs
  violatedPredictions: z.array(z.string()),  // hypothesis IDs
  confidence: ConfidenceSchema,
  notes: z.string().optional(),
  artifacts: z.array(z.string()).optional(),  // Links to data, figures, etc.
});

export type TestRecord = z.infer<typeof TestRecordSchema>;
export type TestExecution = z.infer<typeof TestExecutionSchema>;
export type Prediction = z.infer<typeof PredictionSchema>;
export type TestStatus = z.infer<typeof TestStatusSchema>;
export type Confidence = z.infer<typeof ConfidenceSchema>;
```

### ID Generation
```typescript
export function generateTestId(sessionId: string, sequence: number): string {
  const paddedSeq = sequence.toString().padStart(3, '0');
  return `T-${sessionId}-${paddedSeq}`;
}
```

### Validation
```typescript
export function validateTestRecord(data: unknown): ValidationResult {
  const result = TestRecordSchema.safeParse(data);
  if (result.success) {
    return { valid: true, errors: [] };
  }
  return {
    valid: false,
    errors: result.error.errors.map(e => `${e.path.join('.')}: ${e.message}`),
  };
}

export function validatePredictionConsistency(test: TestRecord): ValidationResult {
  const errors: string[] = [];
  
  // Check that all target hypotheses exist
  // Check that predictions are mutually distinguishable
  // Check that at least 2 hypotheses are targeted (discriminative test)
  
  if (test.targetHypotheses.length < 2) {
    errors.push('Discriminative test should target at least 2 hypotheses');
  }
  
  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors };
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All test-related types defined with Zod
- [ ] ID generation follows pattern
- [ ] Prediction schema links to hypotheses
- [ ] Execution schema captures results and interpretation
- [ ] Validation ensures discriminative test design

## Dependencies
- Feature: Hypothesis Registry Schema (for hypothesis ID format)

## Effort Estimate
~150 lines of code, ~1.5 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:53.766561476-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:46:13.955680805-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:46:13.955680805-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in apps/web/src/lib/schemas/test-record.ts and prediction.ts with comprehensive tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:01.97414199-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-lm3b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:02.159569393-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:41:23.734488729-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-x3xg","title":"FEATURE: Confidence Calibration Tracking - Learn to Estimate Better","description":"
## Track User's Prediction Accuracy Over Time

A key outcome of the Brenner Loop is improving the user's ability to estimate probabilities. This feature tracks calibration and provides feedback.

### What is Calibration?
When you say "80% confident," you should be right 80% of the time. If you're right 50% of the time, you're overconfident. If you're right 95% of the time, you're underconfident.

### Tracking Mechanism
For each locked prediction with stated confidence:
- Record: prediction, confidence (0-100%), outcome (correct/incorrect)
- Accumulate over all predictions
- Calculate: actual accuracy at each confidence level

### Calibration Curve
- X-axis: Stated confidence (0-100%)
- Y-axis: Actual accuracy (0-100%)
- Perfect calibration: 45-degree line
- Show user's actual curve vs. ideal

### Metrics
- **Brier Score**: Mean squared error of probability estimates
- **Calibration Error**: Average deviation from ideal
- **Over/Under-confidence**: Direction of systematic bias
- **Sharpness**: Do they use the full 0-100% range?

### Feedback Loop
- After each prediction resolution, show:
  - "You said 80%, outcome was [correct/incorrect]"
  - "Your calibration at 80%: 65% accurate (overconfident)"
  - Specific advice: "Try being less certain, or gather more evidence first"

### Long-term Progress
- Track calibration improvement over time
- Celebrate when calibration improves
- Identify domains where user is well-calibrated vs. not

### Depends On
- njjo.2 (Confidence Update Algorithm)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-x53","title":"Feature: Search Result Navigation","description":"Enhance the experience of navigating TO and viewing search results.

## Rationale

Finding a result is only half the job. Users need to:
1. Navigate to the exact location
2. See the match in context
3. Understand where they are in the document
4. Easily continue searching or return to results

## Scope

### Deep Linking
- Search result links to exact section anchor
- Scroll to the matched section
- Highlight the matched text temporarily (flash effect)

### Result Context
- After navigation, show breadcrumb of location
- 'Back to search' button
- Remember scroll position if user returns to search

### Multiple Results in Same Document
- If multiple matches in one document, show all
- Jump between matches (next/prev)
- Counter: 'Match 2 of 7'

### Mobile Considerations
- Bottom sheet for search results (already have bottom sheet component)
- Swipe to navigate between matches
- Easy dismiss and return to document

## Deliverables

- Deep link navigation to search results
- Match highlighting in document view
- Multi-match navigation within documents
- 'Back to search' flow

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Clicking result scrolls to exact match
- [ ] Match text highlighted temporarily
- [ ] Can navigate between multiple matches
- [ ] Can return to search results easily
- [ ] Mobile navigation feels natural","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:12.10567957-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:54:57.260866056-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:54:57.260866056-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented: query param in URLs for back-to-search, flash highlight on navigated sections, text highlighting from search terms, and floating Back to Search button. Multi-match navigation deferred for future enhancement.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x53","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-cxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:23.917557812-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x53","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-d4p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:25.123596081-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-x61","title":"Implement progressive loading for mobile transcript (retry)","description":"# Context
The linter keeps reverting progressive loading changes to TranscriptViewer.tsx.

# Implementation (apply manually or disable linter temporarily)

1. Add constants before TranscriptViewer function:
```tsx
const MOBILE_INITIAL_SECTIONS = 8;
const MOBILE_LOAD_INCREMENT = 8;
```

2. Add state and ref in the function:
```tsx
const [mobileSectionsLoaded, setMobileSectionsLoaded] = useState(MOBILE_INITIAL_SECTIONS);
const loadMoreSentinelRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
```

3. Add IntersectionObserver effect

4. Update scrollToSection to load target section first

5. Update mobile rendering to use `.slice(0, mobileSectionsLoaded)` and add sentinel element

See brenner_bot-s8x close reason for full details.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Claude","created_at":"2025-12-30T02:31:20.863684673-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T02:37:24.149298614-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T02:37:24.149298614-05:00","close_reason":"Progressive loading for mobile transcript is implemented - loads 8 sections initially, uses IntersectionObserver to load 8 more as user scrolls, shows loading spinner, and auto-loads target section when scrolling to section not yet rendered.  {"id":"brenner_bot-x712","title":"Task: Navigation & Layout Component Tests","description":"# Goal
Add unit tests for navigation and layout components.

# Components to Test

## 1. nav.tsx (17KB)
Test main navigation:
- Link rendering for all routes
- Active link highlighting
- Mobile menu toggle
- Navigation state persistence
- Keyboard navigation
- Responsive breakpoints

## 2. collapsible.tsx (10KB)
Test collapsible sections:
- Expand/collapse toggle
- Content visibility
- Animation states
- Keyboard accessibility (Enter/Space)
- ARIA attributes
- Nested collapsibles

## 3. bottom-sheet.tsx (5.8KB)
Test mobile bottom sheet:
- Open/close behavior
- Drag to dismiss
- Snap points
- Content scrolling
- Backdrop click to close
- Focus trapping

## 4. comparison-view.tsx (6.4KB)
Test side-by-side comparison:
- Two-panel rendering
- Synchronized scrolling
- Panel resizing
- Full-screen toggle
- Panel swap

## 5. reading-progress-widget.tsx (6.0KB)
Test reading progress indicator:
- Progress calculation
- Visual indicator update
- Section tracking
- Persistence across navigation
- Reset functionality

# Test Pattern
```typescript
// nav.test.tsx
import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react';
import userEvent from '@testing-library/user-event';
import { Nav } from './nav';

describe('Nav', () => {
  it('renders all navigation links', () => {
    render(<Nav />);
    
    expect(screen.getByRole('link', { name: /corpus/i })).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByRole('link', { name: /method/i })).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByRole('link', { name: /sessions/i })).toBeInTheDocument();
  });
  
  it('highlights active link based on current route', () => {
    // Mock usePathname or render with router context
    render(<Nav />, { wrapper: createRouterWrapper('/corpus') });
    
    expect(screen.getByRole('link', { name: /corpus/i }))
      .toHaveAttribute('aria-current', 'page');
  });
  
  it('toggles mobile menu on hamburger click', async () => {
    const user = userEvent.setup();
    // Set viewport to mobile
    render(<Nav />);
    
    await user.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /menu/i }));
    
    expect(screen.getByRole('navigation')).toHaveClass('mobile-open');
  });
});
```

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] nav.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] collapsible.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] bottom-sheet.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] comparison-view.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] reading-progress-widget.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] All ARIA attributes tested
- [ ] Keyboard accessibility tested
- [ ] Mobile/responsive behavior tested","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:51:30.020687914-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T02:03:15.312812826-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T02:03:15.312812826-05:00","close_reason":"Partial completion: Added 106 tests (58 nav + 48 collapsible). Remaining: bottom-sheet, comparison-view, reading-progress-widget can be tracked separately.","labels":["testing unit-tests components layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x712","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8dy3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:36:31.301230662-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
